chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D – 311 of 2026 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1 For orders on office objection 2 For hearing of CMA No.1372/2026 3 For hearing of main case 25.02.2026 Mr. Imamuddin Chandio, Advocate for the Petitioner. M/s. Hakim Ali Shaikh, Addl. A.G. Sindh and Sagheer Ahmed Abbasi, AAG a/w IO/SIP Muhammad Ashraf, SIP/SIO Adalat Khan Wagan and SIP Muhammad Tariq, PS KIA, Karachi. ------------------------------------ Report submitted by The IO/SIP Muhammad Ashraf, PS KIA Karachi, taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that respondent No.5 is the complainant of the impugned FIR and was father-in-law of the petitioner. Due to matrimonial dispute between the petitioner and his wife the marriage / wedlock ended with mutual understanding by way of divorce, however, respondent No.5 being an advocate has managed to get registered false FIR against the petitioner so that he could meet his unjustified demands. The IO/SIP Muhammad Ashraf, PS KIA Karachi present, submits that the veracity of the FIR in question was doubtful, therefore, he after thorough investigation has disposed it of under “B” Class and such report has been submitted before the Court concerned through Assistant District Pubic Prosecutor, East Karachi, which is pending adjudication before the Judicial Magistrate-XIX, East Karachi. Since the purpose of filing instant petition in view of above investigation conducted by the I.O. has been over. As far as the verdict of the Judicial Magistrate is concerned, the Judicial Magistrate is directed to decide the fate of the report submitted by the Investigating Officer within a period of two weeks, after providing an opportunity of hearing to both parties and upon consideration of the investigation papers under intimation to this Court.

Since the purpose of filing instant petition in view of above investigation conducted by the I.O. has been over. As far as the verdict of the Judicial Magistrate is concerned, the Judicial Magistrate is directed to decide the fate of the report submitted by the Investigating Officer within a period of two weeks, after providing an opportunity of hearing to both parties and upon consideration of the investigation papers under intimation to this Court. A copy of this order shall be sent by fax to the Judicial Magistrate-XIX, East Karachi through the Sessions Judge, East Karachi for compliance. Learned MIT-II to ensure compliance. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Azeem
